meta.name: Eternal Documentation Scrolls
meta.description: Logs, evidence capture, version control, file organization, and proof-of-work missions.

tasks.title: Write Daily Mission Log
tasks.subject: Daily Logs
tasks.successCriteria: Log captures work done, blockers, decisions, and next move
tasks.failureCondition: Day ends with no written record
tasks.recoveryMission: Write a 3-bullet retro for the missed day

steps.action: Open your daily log template (Notion, Obsidian, Google Doc) (1 min)
Write today's date and session title (1 min)
Record any blockers, challenges, or stuck points (2 min)
Note 1-2 key decisions made today (2 min)
Write the single most important next action for tomorrow (1 min)
Tag entry with project names for easy filtering (1 min)

tasks.notes: This is the backbone of your proof-of-work system. Future you will thank present you for these logs when writing weekly/monthly reports.

tasks.title: Save Proof-of-Work Evidence Pack
tasks.subject: Evidence Capture
tasks.successCriteria: Screenshots or artifacts stored in correct folder with consistent naming
tasks.failureCondition: Work completed but no evidence saved

steps.action: Identify the output to capture (completed task, milestone, deliverable) (1 min)
Take screenshots of key evidence (3-5 images) (4 min)
Export any files or data needed (3 min)
Rename files using naming standard: YYYY-MM-DD_Project_Description (3 min)
Place files in correct folder (by project/date) (2 min)
Add links to evidence in your daily log or dashboard (2 min)

tasks.notes: Evidence packs protect you from forgetting your progress. One screenshot > ten sentences of description.

tasks.title: Save New Version of Draft or File
tasks.subject: Version Control
tasks.successCriteria: New version saved with correct naming format (v# or date)

steps.action: Open the current draft or file (1 min)
Save new version with format: Filename_v#_YYYY-MM-DD (2 min)
Move old version to archive folder (1 min)
Add brief change log note (what changed in this version) (1 min)

tasks.notes: Never overwrite your work — always version. Future you will need to see what changed and when.
